# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a multi-service application container that runs a web server (port 80), API server (port 8080), database (port 5432), and Redis (port 6379). Expose all necessary ports so the services can communicate. This is for our all-in-one development container.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent container security.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official Python runtime as a parent image 
FROM python:3.7-slim   # Adjust this line according your needs, e.g., use 'python' or another version of the base Image if needed (e.g.: alpine)

# Set environment variables for our app and database server to enable them in a Docker container 
ENV APP_HOME=/app   # Adjust this line according your needs, e.g., use '/home' or another directory path of the base Image if needed (e.g.: /var)
# Environment variable definitions for our app and database server to enable them in a Docker container 
ENV DB_HOST=database    # Use 'dbserver', adjust this line according your needs, e.g., use different hostname or port number of the dbServer if needed (e.g.: my-postgresql)  
# Environment variable definitions for our app and database server to enable them in a Docker container 
ENV DB_PORT=5432    # Use 'dbport', adjust this line according your needs, e.g., use different port number of the dbServer if needed (e.g.: my-postgresql)  
# Environment variable definitions for our app and database server to enable them in a Docker container 
ENV DB_NAME=mydatabase    # Use 'dbname', adjust this line according your needs, e.g., use different name of the dbServer if needed (e.g.: my-postgresql)  
# Environment variable definitions for our app and database server to enable them in a Docker container 
ENV DB_USER=myuser    # Use 'dbusr', adjust this line according your needs, e.g., use different user of the dbServer if needed (e.g.: my-postgresql)  
# Environment variable definitions for our app and database server to enable them in a Docker container 
ENV DB_PASS=mypassword    # Use 'dbpass', adjust this line according your needs, e.g., use different password of the dbServer if needed (e.g.: my-postgresql)  
# Environment variable definitions for our app and database server to enable them in a Docker container 
ENV REDIS_HOST=redis    # Use 'dbserver', adjust this line according your needs, e.g., use different hostname or port number of the dbServer if needed (e.g.: my-postgresql)  
# Environment variable definitions for our app and database server to enable them in a Docker container 
ENV REDIS_PORT=6379    # Use 'dbport', adjust this line according your needs, e.g., use different port number of the dbServer if needed (e.g.: my-postgresql)  
# Environment variable definitions for our app and database server to enable them in a Docker container 
ENV REDIS_PASS=null    # Use 'dbpass', adjust this line according your needs, e.g., use different password of the dbServer if needed (e.g.: my-postgresql)  
# Environment variable definitions for our app and database server to enable them in a Docker container 
ENV REDIS_DB=0    # Use 'dbname', adjust this line according your needs, e.g., use different name of the dbServer if needed (e.g.: my-postgresql)  
# Environment variable definitions for our app and database server to enable them in a Docker container 
ENV REDIS_TLS=false    # Use 'dbusr', adjust this line according your needs, e.g., use different user of the dbServer if needed (e.g.: my-postgresql)  
# Environment variable definitions for our app and database server to enable them in a Docker container
